findings.hypothesis_text: TP53-mutant BLCA tumors show a distinct intra-tumoral immune/stromal cell composition compared to TP53-wildtype tumors.
findings.effect: No significant differences in H&E-derived density: lymphocytes 125.7 vs 211.2, eosinophils 6.1 vs 20.8, fibroblasts 458.0 vs 488.5, plasmocytes 3.0 vs 3.7, neutrophils 10.9 vs 11.5, cancer cells 2236 vs 1929 (TP53-mutant vs wildtype); all Wilcoxon adj. p=1.0
findings.reason: Wilcoxon pairwise comparisons across all six cell-density metrics were non-significant (adj. p=1.0 for every cell type), and the TP53-mutant group (n=3) was too small to provide adequate statistical power to detect true differences.
findings.markdown:
**[INTERNAL]**
### TP53-mutant vs. TP53-wildtype intra-tumoral immune/stromal cell composition in MOSAIC BLCA
- **Disease**: BLCA
- **Status**: abandoned
- **Effect**: No significant differences in density: lymphocytes 125.7 vs 211.2, eosinophils 6.1 vs 20.8, fibroblasts 458.0 vs 488.5, plasmocytes 3.0 vs 3.7, neutrophils 10.9 vs 11.5, cancer cells 2236 vs 1929; all Wilcoxon adj. p=1.0
- **Entities**: TP53
- **Reason**: All six Wilcoxon pairwise comparisons were non-significant (adj. p=1.0) and the TP53-mutant cohort (n=3) was severely underpowered relative to TP53-wildtype (n=12)

Despite numerically higher lymphocyte (211 vs 126) and eosinophil (21 vs 6) densities in TP53-wildtype tumors, no cell-composition metric reached significance in this 15-patient cohort. Validation in a larger cohort (e.g. TCGA-BLCA) is needed before drawing conclusions about TP53-associated immune infiltration patterns.
findings.author: Roopal

findings.hypothesis_text: TP53-mutant BLCA tumors have distinct spatial immune architecture (TIL diffusivity and TLS score) compared to TP53-wildtype tumors.
findings.effect: TIL diffusivity: 0.47 (n=1) vs 0.30±0.06 (n=10), adj. p=0.36; TLS score: 0.60±0.42 (n=3) vs 0.80±0.16 (n=12), adj. p=0.63; neither significant
findings.reason: TIL diffusivity and TLS score comparisons were non-significant (adj. p=0.36 and 0.63 respectively), and the TP53-mutant group was extremely small (n=3, with only n=1 slide for diffusivity), precluding robust detection of spatial architecture differences.
findings.markdown:
**[INTERNAL]**
### TP53-mutant vs. TP53-wildtype spatial immune architecture (TIL diffusivity and TLS score) in MOSAIC BLCA
- **Disease**: BLCA
- **Status**: abandoned
- **Effect**: TIL diffusivity 0.47 (n=1) vs 0.30±0.06 (n=10), adj. p=0.36; TLS score 0.60±0.42 (n=3) vs 0.80±0.16 (n=12), adj. p=0.63; neither significant
- **Entities**: TP53
- **Reason**: Both spatial metrics were non-significant after Wilcoxon adjustment, and the TP53-mutant group was limited to n=3 patients (n=1 for TIL diffusivity), making the comparison severely underpowered

The single TP53-mutant slide shows numerically higher TIL diffusivity (more diffuse infiltration) while TP53-wildtype tumors trend toward higher TLS scores (more organised adaptive immune structures), but neither trend is statistically supported. Larger cohort validation is warranted before attributing spatial immune organisation differences to TP53 status.
findings.author: Roopal
